_comment: Tracks FDA oncology drug approvals beyond our CMS data cutoff (2023). Each drug here is pre-staged in cancerMaps.ts so it will auto-categorize the moment 2024+ CMS data is ingested.

drugs.indication: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (BCMA x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager)
drugs.notes: Late 2023 approval — limited 2023 CMS claims; full representation in 2024 data

drugs.indication: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (GPRC5D x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager)
Relapsed/refractory AML with KMT2A rearrangement or NUP98 rearrangement (menin inhibitor)
drugs.notes: Novel menin inhibitor class — first approval for KMT2A-rearranged AML

drugs.indication: NSCLC (ROS1+), solid tumors with NTRK fusion (TKI-naive and -pretreated)
drugs.notes: Approved for both TKI-naive and post-crizotinib/entrectinib ROS1+ NSCLC

drugs.indication: Extensive-stage SCLC, previously treated (DLL3 x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager)
drugs.notes: First DLL3-targeted therapy; given IV every 2 weeks — Part B billing

drugs.indication: NRG1 fusion-positive NSCLC and pancreatic adenocarcinoma
drugs.mechanism: HER2 x HER3 bispecific antibody; blocks NRG1 signaling
drugs.notes: First FDA-approved therapy for NRG1 fusion+ tumors (tumor-agnostic-like indication)

drugs.indication: HR+/HER2- breast cancer, PIK3CA mutated, post-CDK4/6 inhibitor (in combo with palbociclib + fulvestrant)
drugs.notes: Added to CDK4/6 inhibitor combo regimen; oral Part D drug

drugs.indication: Gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, CLDN18.2+ HER2-negative, 1st line (+ chemo)
drugs.notes: First CLDN18.2-targeted therapy approval in the US; IV infusion

drugs.indication: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, ≥3 prior lines (BCMA x CD3 bispecific)
drugs.notes: Third approved BCMA x CD3 bispecific after teclistamab and elranatamab

drugs.indication: Relapsed/refractory B-cell ALL in adults (CD19 CAR-T)
drugs.notes: 4th CD19 CAR-T; lentiviral vector, lower cytokine release syndrome profile

drugs.indication: NSCLC with EGFR exon 19 del or L858R, 1st line in combination with amivantamab (Rybrevant)
drugs.notes: Only approved in combination with amivantamab (MARIPOSA trial); oral Part D drug

drugs.indication: NSCLC (ROS1+, TKI-naive and post-crizotinib), solid tumors (NTRK+)
drugs.notes: CNS-penetrant ROS1 inhibitor; similar class to repotrectinib

drugs.indication: Blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN), treatment-naive or relapsed
drugs.notes: Rare hematologic malignancy; second approved therapy after tagraxofusp

drugs.indication: HR+/HER2- breast cancer (post-CDK4/6 + endocrine), non-sq NSCLC (no actionable driver, post-platinum)
drugs.notes: Third ADC in the deruxtecan family (after T-DXd and Dato-DXd sharing payload)

drugs.indication: Non-sq NSCLC with c-MET protein overexpression (IHC 3+ or IHC 2+), post-platinum
drugs.notes: Targets c-MET protein overexpression (not c-MET amplification); ~25% of non-sq NSCLC

drugs.indication: NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion (PDUFA target 2025)
drugs.notes: PDUFA date 2025 — pre-staged in map; will appear in claims if approved

drugs.indication: EGFR-mutated NSCLC, post-EGFR TKI and platinum (BLA under FDA review 2025)
drugs.notes: MARIPOSA-2 and HERTHENA-Lung01 trials; BLA under review

howToUpdate.step1: When a new drug is FDA-approved, add it to this file AND to backend/src/cancerMaps.ts DRUG_ENTRIES
howToUpdate.step2: Use both the generic name and the generic-suffix form (e.g. 'ELRANATAMAB' and 'ELRANATAMAB-BCMM') since CMS data may use either
howToUpdate.step3: Set expectedInCmsData to the calendar year after approval (Part D data lags ~12-18 months)
howToUpdate.step4: When the monthly CMS checker opens a GitHub Issue, download the new year's data and run the ETL pipeline
howToUpdate.step5: After ingesting new data, verify the drug appears by searching a known prescriber in the Collaboration Network tab
